Timeline › Policies › Content Moderation

Halyard Pay policy

Content Moderation

11 proposed revisions across 5 of 5 reviewed releases, including 2 breaking changes.

Current policy

policy.md

Content Moderation (BRAM Brand Integrity)

Mastercard's Business Risk Assessment and Mitigation (BRAM) program includes specific brand-integrity provisions that prohibit acquirers from processing transactions for merchants engaged in activities that violate Mastercard's acceptable use standards. Halyard Pay conducts ongoing content monitoring of merchant storefronts and transaction data to identify and remediate prohibited content.

Prohibited categories

Merchants found to be selling or facilitating counterfeit goods, illegal drugs, adult content that violates applicable law, goods or services that infringe intellectual property rights, or gambling services in jurisdictions where such services are restricted are subject to immediate review and, where confirmed, merchant suspension.

Required actions

  1. Review merchant storefront content on a weekly cadence using automated scanning combined with periodic manual sampling.
  2. Flag any merchant whose content matches a prohibited category for human review within one business day.
  3. Suspend the merchant's processing privileges pending investigation if prohibited content is confirmed.
  4. Document all findings and remediation actions in the BRAM case file.

Source authority: Mastercard SPME §10.5.

rules.yaml

program: Content Moderation (BRAM)
authority: Mastercard SPME §10.5
prohibited_categories:
  - counterfeit_goods
  - illegal_drugs
  - adult_content_violations
  - intellectual_property_violations
  - gambling_in_restricted_jurisdictions
review_cadence: weekly
human_review_trigger_business_days: 1
confirmed_violation_action: suspend_processing
case_documentation_required: true
agent_owner: content_mod_agent

Authorized by

SPME §10.5 — BRAM Brand Integrity and Prohibited Merchant Content

Proposed changes across releases

Jun 2022 → May 2023
1 change
breaking §9.4.1 Non-face-to-face Adult Content and Services Merchants
May 2023 → Sep 2023
3 changes
substantive §5.11.7 of the Mastercard Rules manual (“the Illegal or Brand-damaging Transactions Rule”). substantive §9.3 General Monitoring Requirements substantive §9.4.1 Non-face-to-face Adult Content and Services Merchants
Sep 2023 → Feb 2024
1 change
substantive §8.9.1 MMP Participation Requirements
Feb 2024 → Sep 2024
1 change
breaking §10.5 Alternative Acquirer Investigation (AAI) Standards
Sep 2024 → May 2025
5 changes
substantive §10 Not use, or allow the use of, any type of technology or other organization measures that substantive §8.8.4 Noncompliance Assessment Mitigation substantive §8.9.1 MMP Participation Requirements substantive §9.3 General Monitoring Requirements substantive §9.4.1 Non-face-to-face Adult Content and Services Merchants
